stem: A group \( G \) has order [[gord]], and \( H \) is a subgroup of \( G \) with order [[h]]. By Lagrange's theorem, what is the index \( [G:H] \) (the number of left cosets of \( H \) in \( G \))?
distractor_rationales: This restates \( |H| \) instead of dividing \( |G| \) by it.
This restates \( |G| \) instead of computing the index.
This subtracts instead of dividing.
This adds instead of dividing.
This is off by one from the correct index.
This is off by one from the correct index.

stem: In the group \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \), let \( H = \langle [[k]] \rangle \) be the subgroup GENERATED by the element [[k]] (all multiples of [[k]] modulo [[n]]). How many elements does \( H \) contain?
solution: The cyclic subgroup generated by [[k]] in \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \) has order equal to the order of [[k]], namely \( [[n]]/\gcd([[n]],[[k]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( \gcd([[n]],[[k]]) \) itself, not \( n \) divided by it.
This restates the generator [[k]] instead of computing the subgroup's size.
This assumes \( H \) is the whole group.
This subtracts the generator from the modulus for no valid reason.
This is off by one from the correct subgroup order.
This is off by one from the correct subgroup order.

stem: In \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \), let \( H = \langle [[k]] \rangle \) be the cyclic subgroup generated by [[k]]. What is the index \( [\mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} : H] \)?
distractor_rationales: This is \( |H| \) itself, the ORDER of the subgroup, not its index.
This restates the generator [[k]] instead of computing the index.
This assumes \( H \) is trivial, giving the full group order as the index.
This subtracts the index from the modulus for no valid reason.
This is off by one from the correct index.
This is off by one from the correct index.

stem: A cyclic group of order [[n]] has exactly ONE subgroup for each positive divisor of [[n]] (and no others). How many subgroups in total does \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \) have?
solution: The subgroups of \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \) correspond exactly to the positive divisors of [[n]], so the count is \( d([[n]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This restates the group's order instead of counting its divisors.
This is the number of GENERATORS of \( \mathbb{Z}_{[[n]]} \), i.e. \( \varphi([[n]]) \), not the number of subgroups.
This is one less than the group's order, unrelated to the divisor count.
This is off by one from the correct subgroup count.
This is off by one from the correct subgroup count.
This mistakenly doubles the correct subgroup count.
